title: राधाकृष्णाष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री राधाकृष्णाष्टकम् ॥

श्रीकृष्णो राधया साकं विहरन् वृन्दावने वने।
निकुञ्जे रासमण्डल्यां दिव्यरासविहारकृत्॥ १॥

राधा कृष्णं समालिङ्ग्य रासमण्डलमध्यगा।
प्रेमानन्दरसाविष्टा दिव्यभावेन शोभते॥ २॥

वंशीवटतलस्थाय वेणुनादविहारिणे।
गोपीजनमनोहारिणे कृष्णाय तुभ्यं नमः॥ ३॥

राधा माधव माधुर्यं प्रेमामृतरसाश्रयम्।
वृन्दावनविहारस्य सुखं कोऽपि न वेत्ति तत्॥ ४॥

कदम्बतरुमूलस्थं मुरलीवादनप्रियम्।
राधालिङ्गितवामाङ्गं कृष्णं वन्दे जगद्गुरुम्॥ ५॥

रासलीलारसासक्तौ राधाकृष्णौ परस्परम्।
गोपीमण्डलमध्यस्थौ शोभेतां दिव्यविग्रहौ॥ ६॥

राधे कृष्ण कृष्ण राधे राधे कृष्ण कृष्ण राधे।
इति नामजपाद् भक्तिर्जायते प्रेमलक्षणा॥ ७॥

श्रीराधाकृष्णयुगलं प्रेमभक्तिरसाश्रयम्।
सदा सेव्यं सदा ध्येयं सदा वन्द्यं सनातनम्॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्री राधाकृष्णाष्टकम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Radha Krishna Ashtakam ||

Shri-Krishno Radhaya Sakam Viharan Vrindavane Vane |
Nikunje Rasa-mandalyam Divya-rasa-viharakrit || 1 ||

Radha Krishnam Samalingya Rasa-mandala-madhyaga |
Premananda-rasavishta Divya-bhavena Shobhate || 2 ||

Vamshi-vata-talasthaya Venu-nada-viharine |
Gopi-jana-manohaarine Krishnaya Tubhyam Namah || 3 ||

Radha Madhava Madhuryam Premamrita-rasashrayam |
Vrindavana-viharasya Sukham Kopi Na Vetti Tat || 4 ||

Kadamba-taru-moolastham Murali-vadana-priyam |
Radha-lingita-vamangam Krishnam Vande Jagadgurum || 5 ||

Rasa-leela-rasasaktau Radha-Krishnau Parasparam |
Gopi-mandala-madhyasthau Shobhetam Divya-vigrahau || 6 ||

Radhe Krishna Krishna Radhe Radhe Krishna Krishna Radhe |
Iti Nama-japad Bhaktir-jayate Prema-lakshana || 7 ||

Shri Radha-Krishna-yugalam Prema-bhakti-rasashrayam |
Sada Sevyam Sada Dhyeyam Sada Vandyam Sanatanam || 8 ||

|| Iti Shri Radha Krishna Ashtakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
श्रीकृष्ण राधा के साथ वृन्दावन के वन में विहार करते हुए, निकुञ्ज में रासमण्डल में दिव्य रासविहार करते हैं॥ १॥

राधा कृष्ण को आलिंगन कर रासमण्डल के मध्य में प्रेमानन्द रस में विभोर दिव्य भाव से शोभित होती हैं॥ २॥

वंशीवट के नीचे बैठे, वेणुनाद में विहार करने वाले, गोपीजनों का मन हरने वाले कृष्ण को नमस्कार॥ ३॥

राधा-माधव का माधुर्य प्रेमामृत रस का आश्रय है। वृन्दावन विहार का सुख कोई नहीं जानता॥ ४॥

कदम्ब वृक्ष के मूल में स्थित, मुरली बजाने के प्रिय, राधा द्वारा वामांग में आलिंगित जगद्गुरु कृष्ण को वन्दन॥ ५॥
benefits: Invokes the divine love of Radha and Krishna
Traditionally associated with joy, peace, and spiritual bliss
Ideal for recitation during Janmashtami and Radhashtami
Purifies the heart and removes worldly attachments
description: Radha Krishna Ashtakam — राधाकृष्णाष्टकम् — is an eight-verse devotional hymn offered to Lord Krishna. Rooted in Vaishnava Devotional Literature, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to invoke the divine love of Radha and Krishna, purify the heart and remove worldly attachments, and bring joy, peace, and spiritual bliss. This prayer is traditionally recited on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Janmashtami, Radha Ashtami, and Holi to invoke divine grace. At 8 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 6 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Radha Krishna Ashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Radha Krishna Ashtakam and where does it come from?
seoFaqs.answer: The Radha Krishna Ashtakam is an eight-verse devotional hymn celebrating the divine union of Sri Radha and Sri Krishna in their nitya-leela of Vrindavan. The composition belongs to the broader corpus of Vaishnava devotional literature, particularly the Goswami tradition of Vrindavan that crystallised around the writings of Rupa, Sanatana and Jiva Goswami in the 16th century. Several Yugala-Ashtakams exist within this tradition, all sharing the central theme of madhurya-rasa, the sweetness mood in which Radha-Krishna are worshipped as the eternal divine couple at the heart of Vraja-bhakti.

seoFaqs.question: What is meant by Radha Krishna being addressed as Yugala?
seoFaqs.answer: Yugala means the divine couple, treated by Vaishnava acharyas as a single inseparable Brahman-tattva rather than two separate deities. The hymn's sixth verse describes them as Divya-vigrahau, two divine forms standing together at the centre of the gopi-mandala, while the eighth invokes the Yugalam as the sole shelter of prema-bhakti-rasa. Theologically, Radha is hladini-shakti (Krishna's bliss-energy) and Krishna is shaktiman (the energy-holder), so worshipping the Yugala is considered worshipping the complete Brahman in its sweet personal aspect, not two beings.

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite the Radha Krishna Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: Brahma-muhurta in the early morning, when Vraja-bhakti tradition holds that the nitya-leela of Radha-Krishna is in its niblesh-leela (intimate pre-dawn pastimes), is considered most auspicious. Ekadashi, Janmashtami, Radhashtami (Bhadrapada Shukla Ashtami), Holi and the four months of Kartik are specifically prescribed. Wednesday (Krishna's day) and Friday (Radha's day) are weekly recommendations. Devotees following the Goswami tradition recite the Ashtakam at the four daily seva timings: mangala, sringara, bhoga and shayan. There are no caste, gender or initiation restrictions for this recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What spiritual benefit does the Radha Krishna Ashtakam grant?
seoFaqs.answer: The seventh verse declares that simply chanting the names Radhe Krishna Krishna Radhe causes prema-bhakti, the highest form of devotion characterised by spontaneous love for the divine, to arise in the heart. This is held by Vaishnava acharyas to be the supreme phala (fruit) above moksha itself, since prema-bhakti continues eternally in the spiritual world. The hymn also bestows purification of the heart from worldly attachments, transcendent peace and bliss, eligibility for entry into the Vrindavan-leela in spiritual perception, and ultimately nitya-vraja-vasa, eternal residence in the spiritual Vrindavan.

seoFaqs.question: How is the Radha Krishna Ashtakam different from the Krishna Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: A Krishna Ashtakam (such as the famous one beginning Vasudeva-sutam Devam) focuses on Krishna alone, often emphasising his cosmic Vishnu-aspect, his divine play (lila), or his Bhagavata-Purana narratives such as Govardhan-dharana and Kaliya-mardana. The Radha Krishna Ashtakam centres on the bridal-mood (madhurya-rasa) where Krishna is worshipped together with Radha as his essential complement, and the focus is on rasa-mandala, kunja-vihara and prema-rasa rather than on Krishna's heroic deeds. The Yugala-Ashtakam is therefore the preferred recitation for sadhakas of the Gaudiya, Nimbarka and Pushti sampradayas.

seoFaqs.question: Why is Radha invoked before Krishna in the chant Radhe Krishna?
seoFaqs.answer: Vaishnava acharyas of the Gaudiya and Nimbarka traditions explain that Radha, as the supreme devotee and the embodiment of Krishna's prema-shakti, is the granter of access to Krishna himself. Chanting Radhe before Krishna is therefore an acknowledgment that one approaches Krishna only through the grace of Radha, not directly. This is the same spiritual logic by which devotees say Sita-Ram and Lakshmi-Narayan before the Lord's name. The seventh verse of this Ashtakam, Radhe Krishna Krishna Radhe Radhe Krishna Krishna Radhe, weaves both names so that the awareness of Yugala-tattva is constantly maintained in the chanting.
